# UC-11.1.7 · Admin Activity Audit

## Description

M365 admin actions (user creation, license changes, policy modifications) need audit trails for compliance and security.

## Value

M365 admin actions (user creation, license changes, policy modifications) need audit trails for compliance and security.

## Implementation

Ingest O365 admin audit log. Track admin operations by administrator. Alert on sensitive operations (user creation, role changes, policy modifications). Correlate with change management tickets.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_MS_O365`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: O365 audit log (admin operations).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest O365 admin audit log. Track admin operations by administrator. Alert on sensitive operations (user creation, role changes, policy modifications). Correlate with change management tickets.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" RecordType=1
| table _time, UserId, Operation, ObjectId, ResultStatus
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Admin Activity Audit** — M365 admin actions (user creation, license changes, policy modifications) need audit trails for compliance and security.

Documented **Data sources**: O365 audit log (admin operations). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_MS_O365`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: o365; **sourcetype**: ms:o365:management. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=o365, sourcetype="ms:o365:management". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Pipeline stage (see **Admin Activity Audit**): table _time, UserId, Operation, ObjectId, ResultStatus
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Exchange Admin Center, Microsoft 365 Defender portal, and message trace for the same messages and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (admin activities), Timeline (admin events), Bar chart (actions by admin).

## SPL

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" RecordType=1
| table _time, UserId, Operation, ObjectId, ResultStatus
| sort -_time
```

## Visualization

Table (admin activities), Timeline (admin events), Bar chart (actions by admin).
